homepage.heroSubtitle: Open Toon Blast in your browser and start a focused game session without installing an app from this site.
homepage.lede: Toon Blast is a browser block puzzle where you tap matching color groups, clear objectives, and manage limited moves with a little planning.

about.headline: A straightforward match puzzle for browser play.
about.paragraph1: Toon Blast is a browser block puzzle where you tap matching color groups, clear objectives, and manage limited moves with a little planning.
about.paragraph2: The site keeps the experience simple: open the Play page, let the local game wrapper load, read the on-screen cues, and play at your own pace. No account is required from this site.

howToPlay.subheading: Learn the objective, make deliberate moves, and improve through repeat attempts.

steps.title: Open the Game

faq.q: Can I play Toon Blast online?
faq.a: Yes. This site opens Toon Blast through a local browser game page at /game/index.html.

faq.q: Do I need to download Toon Blast?
faq.a: No app download is required from this site. The game loads in an embedded browser window.

faq.q: Is Toon Blast an official app page?
faq.a: No. This is an independent browser game page that links to an external playable embed source.

faq.q: Does the game work on mobile?
faq.a: The embedded game is intended for browser play and may work on mobile devices that support the source iframe.

faq.q: What should I do if the game does not load?
faq.a: Refresh the page, check your connection, and make sure your browser allows embedded game frames.

faq.q: What is the best first tip for Toon Blast?
faq.a: Look for larger groups before tapping. Saving a bigger match can create a cleaner board than clearing small pairs immediately.

playPage.howItWorksSubtitle: Three simple steps to start playing

steps.title: Open Toon Blast
steps.desc: Visit the Play page and wait for the game frame to finish loading.

steps.title: Learn the Controls
steps.desc: Use the first moments to understand clicks, taps, turns, or prompts shown inside the game.

steps.title: Play and Adjust
steps.desc: Replay with one clear improvement in mind instead of rushing every move.

items.a: Toon Blast is a browser block puzzle where you tap matching color groups, clear objectives, and manage limited moves with a little planning.

items.q: How do I start Toon Blast?
items.a: Open the Play page, wait for the iframe to load, and follow the first visible prompt inside the game.

items.q: Does this page require an account?
items.a: This site does not require an account to open the game page.

items.q: Is there a guaranteed score or reward?
items.a: No. The page provides casual gameplay access and practical tips only; it does not promise rewards, prizes, or scores.

items.q: Why is the game inside a local wrapper?
items.a: The local wrapper keeps the site route consistent while the external playable source is recorded separately for tracking.
The copy is written for a general audience, but parents should still supervise web game use and third-party embeds.

howToPlayPage.intro: Toon Blast is easiest to enjoy when you slow down for the first attempt, read the screen, and learn what the game asks for before trying to move faster.

steps.title: Load the Game
steps.body: Use the Play page and give the external iframe a moment to appear.

steps.title: Check the Objective
steps.body: Look for the current target, board condition, timer, move limit, or turn prompt.

steps.title: Practice the Input
steps.body: Make a few careful taps, clicks, or turns so the controls feel predictable.

steps.title: Plan Before Acting
steps.body: Look for larger groups before tapping. Saving a bigger match can create a cleaner board than clearing small pairs immediately.

steps.title: Review Each Attempt
steps.body: After a round ends, notice what caused trouble and change that single habit next time.

howToPlayPage.mechanic: Tap connected blocks of the same color, watch the move limit, and use the first round to learn how objectives are shown.
howToPlayPage.difficultyProgression: The challenge comes from making cleaner decisions as the screen becomes busier or the available choices become tighter.
tips: Look for larger groups before tapping. Saving a bigger match can create a cleaner board than clearing small pairs immediately.
Avoid rushing the first move before you understand the current objective.
Keep your browser focused on the game frame so clicks and taps register correctly.
If performance feels slow, close extra tabs and reload the Play page.
